title: 3-Bedroom Houses for Rent in Kamm's Corners, Cleveland

answerBox: Kamm's Corners is a commercial and residential node within Cleveland's West Park district, centered on Lorain Avenue and Rocky River Drive, with larger streetcar-suburb lots that often accommodate 3-bedroom single-family homes. Cleveland's citywide 3-bedroom median rent was $1,350/month per Zumper's July 2026 report.

body: Kamm's Corners is the commercial hub at Lorain Avenue and Rocky River Drive on Cleveland's far west side, within the larger West Park statistical planning area near the city's border with Fairview Park. Because West Park developed later as a streetcar suburb rather than a 19th-century urban district, lots here tend to be larger than in Cleveland's near-west neighborhoods, which more often allows for a three-bedroom layout with a full yard and driveway.

Rent Finder Cleveland's managed portfolio is concentrated on Cleveland's east and southeast side, so our current three-bedroom listings directly near Kamm's Corners are limited. [Tell our team what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) and we'll point you to current three-bedroom options across the areas we serve.

sections.h2: What does a 3-bedroom rent for near Kamm's Corners?
body: There's no rent index specific to Kamm's Corners, so citywide Cleveland data is the closest available benchmark. Zumper's July 4, 2026 report puts the citywide 3-bedroom median at $1,350/month, while RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report on professionally-managed apartment buildings shows a much higher $2,716 for a 3-bedroom — a gap that reflects newer, larger complexes rather than the single-family housing stock typical of West Park.

sections.h2: Housing stock: finding a 3-bedroom near Kamm's Corners
body: Most homes near Kamm's Corners are single-family houses built in the early-to-mid 20th century, generally with front porches, attached or detached garages, and yards larger than those found in Cleveland's tighter near-west grids like Ohio City or Clark-Fulton. Three-bedroom configurations are common in this housing stock, typically arranged across two floors with bedrooms upstairs and living space on the main level.

items: **Location:** Commercial node at Lorain Ave & Rocky River Dr, within West Park
**Layout:** Bedrooms typically upstairs, living space on the main floor

body: GCRTA bus routes run along Lorain Avenue and Rocky River Drive directly through the Kamm's Corners business district, connecting to downtown Cleveland and neighboring west-side suburbs, per RTA's published route network. The area is also close to I-71, giving drivers a quick route toward downtown or south toward Cleveland Hopkins International Airport.

sections.h2: Section 8 vouchers for 3-bedroom rentals near Kamm's Corners
body: Acceptance of Housing Choice Vouchers on any specific home near Kamm's Corners depends on the individual landlord, since Ohio has no statewide requirement and Cleveland has no source-of-income protection ordinance. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages, in any Cleveland neighborhood, accepts vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready — our current inventory leans toward the east and southeast side, so availability directly in West Park will vary. Vouchers themselves are issued by the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA), which accepts preliminary applications year-round through its online portal and runs selection through periodic lottery draws rather than strict first-come-first-served. Read our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for the full CMHA process, or check our [cheap houses near Kamm's Corners guide](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/cheap-houses-for-rent-kamm-s-corners-cleveland/) if budget matters more than bedroom count.

sections.h2: What to check before renting an older 3-bedroom near Kamm's Corners
body: Many of the single-family houses in the West Park area predate 1978, which brings them under Cleveland's Codified Ordinances Chapter 365 — the city's **Lead-Safe Certification** requirement for residential rental units built before January 1, 1978. The certification is valid for two years and follows a visual and dust-wipe evaluation by a certified risk assessor, so it's reasonable to ask a landlord for the current certificate before signing a lease on an older 3-bedroom house.

It's also worth confirming Ohio's security-deposit rules apply the same way here as anywhere else in the state: under Ohio Revised Code 5321.16, a landlord generally must return a deposit within 30 days of move-out with an itemized list of deductions, and owes 5% annual interest on any amount over $50 or one month's rent if the tenant stayed six months or longer. This is general information, not legal advice, so confirm current statute details before signing.

faq.a: Acceptance depends on each individual landlord, since Ohio has no statewide requirement and Cleveland has no source-of-income ordinance. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages, regardless of neighborhood, accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready.
